clean_text: Alhamdulillah. Continuing in the chapter of vigilance, vigilance of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala's watching, vigilance of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala's listening to us, vigilance of Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala's knowledge, having constant knowledge and awareness that Allah subhanaha is above us watching us and observing. we stopped at the narration of and we started the summarized biography of basically we're going to mention when we come across a narrator a companion we're going to try to keep it as concise as possible so that we're able to memorize it But we want it to also be more than just a few footnotes. Otherwise, we will start to confuse all of these names. We haven't met these people. We're trying to get to know them as if we have met them. And so, memorizing just random names without anything that differentiates them from one another is very difficult. So, we want them to be more than just names that we've heard of before. We want to know who these men were. We started with Mujab. What is Muabel's? Abu what? Huh? Abu Abdman. Yes. Abu Abdman. Can we think of anybody else who has that same? Okay. Well, me. Yeah. of the companions. Very good. Another one that we all know. We just listened to his father's narration a couple days ago. Omar. Okay. So there's threebas all is one of the ansar he's ansari but what's unique about jabal is that he actually became Muslim before hijra before the prophet sallallahu alaihi wasallam arrived at medina as a matter of fact, he was one of the people that pledged allegiance to him while he was still in Mecca and helped to facilitate his hijra from Mecca to Medina and he was one of the companions to receive him. He was there in is when the came for Hajj and they met at an area called and they pledged allegiance to the prophet sallall.aii wasallam there that if he came or when he would come to Medina that they would protect him and so forth. And so the tribe leaders and the Muslim were there in Medina to pledge their allegiance to him. Now there are two in this happened twice. You have the major and minor was at the second of those. He became Muslim when he was 18 years old. Very young man and he as we mentioned before accepted Islam and was preparing for the prophet sallallah.aii's arrival in Medina. Another very important point about Muad is that he was in the battle of Badr. And the battle of Badr is the most significant battle in all of the history of Islam. And the people that were in the battle of Badr, the companions that were at the battle of Badr, 312 or 13 or 14 or 15, the scholars differ on exact numbers. All of them have been guaranteed forgiveness by Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala that Allah subhanahu wa'ta'ala he looked at the people he looked upon the members of the battle of Badr and he said do as you will for I have forgiven Now all of the unsaw are one of two tribes. Do we know these two tribes? What their the names of these two tribes are? Al are more virtuous than Alaz in a general sense. And in the battle of Badr only members of Al were at the battle of Badr. Remember the battle of Badr was voluntary. As a matter of fact, no one had any idea that it was going to even be a battle until they were already at Badr and the armies were all but lining up to face one another. So it was a surprise to everyone including the prophet sallall.aii wasallam. And so it was voluntary. It was considered a raid. It was a caravan that they were going to intercept. That's all it was supposed to be. It turned into a battle and the companions stood firm. That's why there were so few of them. That's why they were not armed in the way that was armed. And so is one of any companion who is from the and was in the battle of Badr, we know automatically he is from which tribe? Als remember these two tribes because these two tribes were at each other's throats for generations before the prophet arrived in Medina. sallallahu alaihi wasallam. They were in constant competition with with each other and they were in constant repraisals with one another, taking casualties on both sides, fighting at every opportunity that presented itself with one another. And so is one of we said Muad became Muslim when he was how old? 18. Okay, here's another fact to help you remember. He passed away in the year 18. Okay, so he became Muslim when he was 18. He passed away in the year 18 after hijb was sent by the prophet sallall.aii Wasallam to Yemen. He was sent to call the people of Yemen to Islam but not all of Yemen. Yemen is a very big place. Yemen was divided and even till recent times till the the the days of the collapse of the Soviet Union. So in the 80s late 80s Yemen was always divided into two sides. the north and the south. And was sent to the region of the region of he was to preside over that region. The other region Abu Mus Ashi ali a means that he's Yemen. Ashi the name Ashari means Yemen from Yemen. It's a tribe from Yemen. Okay. He was sent to preside over the other half. Mu Jabel was sent in the year 10 after Hij just a few months before the prophet sallall.aii wasallam passed away. Now the prophet sallallah.aii alaihi wasallam walked Muad out to the outskirts of Medina to walk him out for him to start his journey. He you know he escorted him out to an area that used to be called Have we heard of? I think we heard of it because of the song that people would sing when they were kids. Kiss kiss and they call it kiss and farewell or something. Whatever. with that. It's the place where people would see the departures of their loved ones. You don't have to have a ticket to be in that area, right? It's where everybody says goodbye. summary: This lecture continues the chapter on vigilance (taqwa) of Allah from what appears to be a hadith collection study. The speaker provides a detailed biographical overview of Muad ibn Jabal, a prominent companion of the Prophet Muhammad. Key details include Muad becoming Muslim at age 18 before the Hijra, his participation in the Battle of Badr (where he received Allah's forgiveness), his tribe affiliation (Al-Khazraj), and his appointment by the Prophet to spread Islam in Yemen. The speaker emphasizes Muad's scholarly virtues and his death at age 35-36 in the 18th year of Hijra during the plague of Amwas in Syria. The lecture employs mnemonic devices and comparative analysis to help students memorize biographical details and understand the significance of companions in Islamic history.
suggested_title: Biography of Muad ibn Jabal: A Companion's Life and Scholarly Virtues
